title: lomatium triternatum var. macrocarpum
intro: Lomatium triternatum var. macrocarpum, also known as Macrocarpum Lomatium, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to the northwestern United States and southwestern Canada. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Lomatium triternatum var. macrocarpum are said to provide relief from various health issues, including colds, flu, and respiratory infections, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
